title: Oncoexapted TEs are enriched in the Active surveillance mode (raw OR=3.88) and the enrichment survives GC-matched stratified permutation

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Oncoexapted TEs are enriched in the Active surveillance mode (raw OR=3.88) and t'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: Per-TE surveillance mode classification from exp03: identity_dependent (H3K9me3) / composition_dependent (H3K27me3) / active / quiescent / bivalent.
TE oncoexaptation flag from Jang et al. 2019 — TEs aberrantly used as alternative promoters/exons in cancer.

operations.note: Stratified permutation: shuffle is_oncoexapted labels WITHIN each GC bin (0.02-wide). Preserves GC distribution exactly; tests whether mode-membership predicts oncoexaptation BEYOND GC.

statistics.name: fisher_odds_ratio_raw
statistics.note: Raw OR — the headline. Active-mode TEs are 3.88x enriched for oncoexaptation.

statistics.name: gc_matched_permutation_or_median
statistics.note: Median OR under 10,000 GC-bin-stratified permutations. Less than raw 3.88 (GC absorbs ~30% of the apparent enrichment) but still substantial.

statistics.name: gc_matched_permutation_p
statistics.note: Empirical p from 10,000 GC-bin-stratified shuffles — fraction of permuted ORs >= raw 3.88.

inference.justification: Three conjuncts: (1) raw OR > 2.0 establishes substantive enrichment; (2) GC-matched permutation OR > 1.5 establishes that the enrichment survives GC matching (some attenuation expected since GC absorbs part of the signal); (3) GC-matched permutation p < 0.001 establishes statistical significance against the GC-controlled null. The MOD-3 pre-registered acceptance was 'PASS at >=7 of 9 mode-marker combinations'; this single combination (active x oncoexaptation) is in the PASS subset.
inference.failure_mode: If s_gc_matched_or fell below 1.5, the apparent enrichment would be attributable mostly to GC; the active-mode-specific oncoexaptation framing would weaken to 'GC-rich TEs are oncoexapted' (true but less informative).

conclusion.assertion: Among 5,006,091 autosomal TEs, those classified into the 'Active' surveillance mode (per exp03 5-class collapse) are enriched 3.88-fold for oncoexaptation (Jang et al. 2019, p = 3.3e-15). The enrichment survives 10,000-permutation stratified shuffling within 0.02-wide GC bins (GC-matched OR = 2.71, permutation p = 1e-4) — TE class membership in the Active mode predicts oncoexaptation BEYOND what GC alone would explain.

scope.scale_note: One of the 9 mode-marker combinations in MOD-3. CoRSIV x identity-dependent and Retro-Age x identity-dependent are sibling claims (also encoded under matched-permutation null).

composite_confidence.note: 10,000-rep stratified permutation under MOD-3 protocol; CI not yet computed.

external_assumptions.statement: Jang 2019 oncoexaptation flag is a defensible binary annotation — TEs aberrantly used as cancer-specific promoters/exons. Annotation is conservative.
0.02-wide GC binning is sufficient to control GC at meaningful resolution; finer binning would reduce within-stratum n and increase permutation variance.
